title: thermopsis montana var. venosa
intro: Thermopsis montana var. venosa, also known as Mountain Foothill Lupine or Western Lupine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the pea family and is native to the mountainous regions of North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it a potential treatment for respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Thermopsis montana var. venosa include its ability to relieve coughs, reduce fever, and ease congestion, making it a popular remedy for colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant typically involve making teas or infusions from the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
